articles.title: 'Trump Pardoned Man Who Sexually Assaulted 12-year-Olds': Jamie Raskin Unleashes Fury On POTUS & ICE
articles.description: Rep. Jamie Raskin launched a blistering attack on ICE during a heated congressional hearing, accusing the agency of fatally shooting American citizens “without any rational justification at all.” The Maryland Democrat argued that federal immigration authorities had exceeded their mandate and demanded accountability for controversial enforcement actions that have drawn national scrutiny. Raskin's remarks came as lawmakers clashed over ICE's conduct, use of force, and oversight mechanisms, turning the hearing into a fierce debate over civil liberties, public safety, and the role of federal immigration enforcement. His comments underscored growing political divisions over the agency's operations and leadership. He also lashed out at Trump for pardoning criminals accused of heinous crimes. Watch.

articles.title: Chip Roy Brutally Grills SPLC Boss: ‘How Many Leftists, Anti-Jewish, Islamists Have You Listed?’
articles.description: A heated exchange unfolded during a House Judiciary Committee hearing as Rep. Chip Roy sharply questioned SPLC President Bryan Fair over the organization's criteria for labeling extremist groups. Roy pressed Fair on how many left-wing organizations, anti-Jewish groups, and Islamist movements had been included in the SPLC's listings, arguing that the group disproportionately focuses on conservatives while overlooking threats from other ideological backgrounds. The Texas Republican repeatedly challenged the organization's consistency and credibility, demanding specific answers about its methodology and classifications. The confrontation highlighted ongoing Republican criticism of the SPLC and broader debates over extremism, political bias, and free speech. Watch.

articles.title: 'Todd Blanche A Disgrace, Real Fraud': Jamie Raskin Unleashes During SPLC Hearing
articles.description: Tempers flared during a contentious congressional hearing as Democratic Representative Jamie Raskin launched a blistering attack on senior Justice Department officials, singling out Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche and accusing him of undermining the department's integrity. Raskin described Blanche as “a disgrace to the DOJ” and “a real fraud,” while arguing that right-wing extremists had been aided and abetted by political allies and sympathetic actors. In a fiery exchange, the Maryland Democrat broadened his criticism, declaring that “all of them” were a disgrace, as lawmakers clashed over extremism, political violence, and the Justice Department’s handling of controversial groups and investigations.

articles.title: Major Win For Indians As U.S. Court Blocks Trump's $100,000 H-1B Visa Fee Hike
articles.description: A major relief for Indian professionals seeking opportunities in the United States. A federal court has struck down the Trump administration's controversial $100,000 H-1B visa extension fee, ruling that such a measure required Congressional approval and could not be imposed through an executive order. Since Indian tech workers account for nearly 70% of H-1B visa beneficiaries, the decision is being seen as a significant win for India's skilled workforce. Meanwhile, the U.S. is also preparing to launch a premium expedited B-1/B-2 visa service, allowing applicants to secure interview appointments within 10 days for an additional fee of $750. The move comes amid broader immigration reforms aimed at tightening oversight while streamlining visa processing. Watch for the full breakdown of what these changes mean for Indian professionals, U.S. employers, and the future of America's immigration policy.

articles.title: Iran-Israel War, Yet No $200 Oil | Why Crude Hasn't Exploded Despite West Asia Tensions
articles.description: Despite months of conflict between Iran and Israel and disruptions around the Strait of Hormuz, oil prices have remained below the feared $100-$200 range. China's reduced crude imports, OPEC+ supply increases and repeated ceasefire signals have helped cap prices. Here's why the global oil market hasn't panicked yet—and why India is watching closely.

This comes after TMC leader Kirti Azad dismissed reports of a separate group of MPs supporting the NDA. Azad called the claims fake and stated Operation Lotus has failed.

articles.title: The Mahabharat War Code: Ancient Secrets Behind Today’s Global Conflicts | ft. Ami Ganatra
articles.description: The Mahabharat is far more than an ancient epic ...it is a timeless guide to understanding power, strategy, leadership, ethics, and conflict. In this fascinating conversation, we explore how the principles and war strategies described in the Mahabharat continue to resonate in today’s geopolitical landscape and global conflicts. Are nations still making the same mistakes that led to the war of Kurukshetra? What can modern leaders learn from the decisions of Krishna, Arjuna, Bhishma, and Duryodhana? Join us as we decode the enduring relevance of the Mahabharat and uncover lessons that remain strikingly relevant in an increasingly complex world. ft. Ami Ganatra#timesmantra #mahabharat #amiganatra #kurukshetra #geopolitics #ancientwisdom #indianepics #worldhistory #leadership #strategythinking

articles.title: Ben Stokes nightclub controversy: What happened between England captain, Gus Atkinson and rugby player?
articles.description: Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson are under ECB investigation after breaching a midnight curfew and becoming involved in a nightclub altercation in Chelsea. Reports claim a Saracens academy rugby player threw a punch at Atkinson but hit an ECB security guard, who needed stitches. ECB sources insist the cricketers “were not the aggressors.” Stokes’s captaincy is now under scrutiny.

articles.title: Manipur DGP visits Kangpokpi, Senapati as fresh protests, highway blockade and census opposition intensify
articles.description: Manipur's new DGP Mukesh Singh visited Kangpokpi and Senapati districts to assess the law and order situation. He assured impartial police action and urged peaceful coexistence. Meanwhile, vehicular movement on NH-202 is suspended due to protests against alleged excessive force on women. A rally also demanded the postponement of the census exercise, citing displacement of conflict-affected people.
